content: 2024–2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as a recursive "agent within an agent" architecture, where the memory system itself acts as an independent curiosity-driven agent that selects which new experiences to encode instead of passively storing inputs from the main task agent. This memory-as-agent design outperforms traditional fixed active learning selection criteria by adapting its encoding priority to the main agent’s long-term task drift.
